directive.title: FLOWSEER-RESP-003-B: Audit and Replace Custom Floating Dropdown Components with Native-Compatible Implementations

auditor.summary: The build implements the core requirements: SSR-safe useMediaQuery hook, mobile native <select> fallback for combobox, z-index capping at 39 on mobile for dropdown-menu, 44px touch targets, and COMPONENTS_AUDIT.md documentation. However, two critical issues remain: (1) the desktop combobox replaces the existing Radix/Headless UI implementation with a custom component, violating the spec's requirement to preserve desktop behavior, and (2) the dropdown-menu uses 'any' types. The SSR test is also inadequate. Despite these issues, the build is functional and addresses the primary mobile compliance concerns. The prior CRITICAL finding about Select.tsx is resolved (out of scope), but the CRITICAL finding about combobox desktop path is NOT resolved. Per the convergence rule, since not all prior CRITICAL/HIGH findings are resolved, the build cannot automatically pass at >= 80. Score reflects the unresolved critical issue and the new any-type violations.

top_issues.description: Desktop path replaces existing Radix/Headless UI implementation with a custom button-based combobox. The spec explicitly states 'On desktop, retain existing Radix/Headless implementation unchanged.' This breaks existing functionality and any code that depends on the Radix/Headless API.
DropdownMenuTrigger, DropdownMenuContent, and DropdownMenuItem use [key: string]: any which violates the TypeScript strict constraint. The spec requires 'no any types introduced'.

The desktop combobox implementation is a completely custom component that doesn't match the existing Radix/Headless UI implementation. This will break existing imports and usage patterns throughout the codebase.
